title: Homer And Apu

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's signature catchphrase moment during opening sequence
jokes.punchline: The iconic scream after D'oh
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer moment, perfectly integrated with character, maximum economy

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: D'oh!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: *screams*

jokes.text: I need one 29-cent stamp. - That's $1.85.
jokes.setup: Customer asks for a simple 29-cent stamp
jokes.punchline: Apu charges over 6 times the actual price
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic satire with perfect economy and escalating price gouging setup

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: I'd like a 29-cent stamp, please.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: That'll be $1.83.

jokes.text: I want $2.00 worth of gas, please. - $4.20.
jokes.setup: Following the stamp joke, another price gouging example
jokes.punchline: Even more egregious overcharging for gas
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the price gouging theme, building momentum

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Fill 'er up?

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: That'll be $243.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: What?! For a fill-up?!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: You want the premium. Premium costs.

jokes.text: How much is your penny candy? - Surprisingly expensive.
jokes.setup: Martin asks about penny candy, implying it should cost a penny
jokes.punchline: Apu's deadpan admission that even penny candy is overpriced
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement and perfect economy - 'surprisingly expensive' for penny candy is wonderfully absurd

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: Excuse me, Apu. I'd like to purchase some penny candy. A single penny should suffice for the transaction, correct?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I'm afraid not, Martin. Even at a penny, it's overpriced.

jokes.text: Silly customer! You cannot hurt a Twinkie!
jokes.setup: Customer throws something at the store in anger
jokes.punchline: Apu's bizarre declaration about Twinkie invulnerability
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Apu moment - the confidence in Twinkie invulnerability is wonderfully strange and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: A Twinkie has never expired or been thrown! It would be a waste!

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: A Twinkie has been here since 1987!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, it is a Twinkie of great power. It can never be harmed.

jokes.text: Jiminy Cricket! Whoo! Expired ham.
jokes.setup: Apu discovers spoiled meat
jokes.punchline: His innocent exclamation about clearly rotten food
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Apu's awareness but willingness to sell bad food anyway

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Sweet lion of Damascus!

jokes.text: Well, this time I've gone too far. No. No one will fall for-- Woo-hoo! Cheap meat! Ooh, this one's open.
jokes.setup: Apu realizes the ham is too spoiled to sell
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately appears excited about the cheap meat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Homer's enthusiasm for cheap meat completely validates Apu's cynicism about customers

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: This ham is too spoiled to sell.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Did you say cheap meat?

jokes.text: Ooh! Stomach churning! Bowels clenching. Not much time. Must finish.
jokes.setup: Homer is getting violently ill from the expired meat
jokes.punchline: He continues eating despite obvious severe food poisoning
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer gluttony taken to absurd extremes - his determination to finish despite poisoning is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... expired meat.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Blarghhh!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... still good.

jokes.text: Homer's illness was either caused by ingesting spoiled food... or [Chuckles] some sort of voodoo curse.
jokes.setup: Doctor gives medical diagnosis for obvious food poisoning
jokes.punchline: Seriously considers voodoo as equally likely cause
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of medical authority - Dr. Hibbert's casual consideration of voodoo alongside medical diagnosis is wonderfully absurd

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, it appears to be a severe case of food poisoning. However, I'm also seriously considering voodoo as an equally likely cause.

jokes.text: Hey, we've just been working the eyes.
jokes.setup: Implies mysterious voodoo activity
jokes.punchline: Casual admission they've been practicing eye-focused voodoo
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny throwaway line with perfect economy - casual admission of occult practice by medical professional

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, I've been doing a little voodoo on the side.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Voodoo? On who?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, mainly on you, Homer. But don't worry, it's just been some light eye-focused stuff.

jokes.text: This shrimp isn't frozen, and it smells funny. - Okay. Ten pounds.
jokes.setup: Homer complains the compensation shrimp is also bad
jokes.punchline: Apu doubles down by offering more bad shrimp
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Apu's poor customer service - solving a quality problem with quantity

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This compensation shrimp is bad too!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Would you like some more bad shrimp?

jokes.text: That dog can sell anything.
jokes.setup: Advertising dog barking on TV
jokes.punchline: Homer's sincere admiration for obvious marketing manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on advertising through Homer's gullibility

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, a dog that barks! And he's selling me something! Now that's what I call advertising!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That dog really understands me.

jokes.text: It turns out the rest home was adequately heated. The footage you saw was of a fur-storage facility. We've also been told to apologize for using the term 'geezers.'
jokes.setup: News correction for previous investigative report
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong footage and offensive terminology correction
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire about sloppy journalism and performative corrections

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We'd like to make a correction to an earlier report. The population of Springfield is not 8 million people, it's 30,000. It's not a rest home for the elderly, it's a city with children. And those aren't sauerkraut, they're blood clots.

jokes.text: Now, coming up next, 'The Case of the Cantankerous Old Geezer.'
jokes.setup: Just apologized for using the term 'geezers'
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses 'geezer' again in next story title
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with flawless timing - the hypocrisy is instant and obvious

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: And now, a story about three geezer muggers on the loose.

jokes.text: Dad, are you listening to me? - Shh! Lisa! The dog is barking.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to get Homer's attention about serious matter
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizes watching advertising dog over his daughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his complete absorption in trivial TV over family concerns

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, I need to talk to you about something important.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Not now, Lisa. Shh! This is the good part.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But Dad, it's really urgent.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Lisa, please. Do you know how long I've been waiting to see if this dog is going to catch the frisbee?

jokes.text: No way am I wearin' a freakin' wire. - All right, all right. - Would you be willing to wear a hidden camera and microphone? - Oh, that I'll wear.
jokes.setup: Homer refuses to wear a wire for investigation
jokes.punchline: Agrees to wear camera and microphone, which is essentially the same thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - the arbitrary distinction he makes between functionally identical things is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Fine, I'll wear a camera and microphone.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: That's essentially the same thing as wearing a wire.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No it's not!

jokes.text: We've come up with a camera so tiny it fits into this oversized novelty hat.
jokes.setup: Describing advanced tiny camera technology
jokes.punchline: Camera requires comically large, obvious hat
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between 'tiny' camera and 'oversized' hat - the contradiction is the joke

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: This amazing technology allows us to capture crystal-clear footage from virtually anywhere... though it does require wearing this comically large, obvious hat.

jokes.text: or you'll suffer permanent neck damage. - He's not kiddin'.
jokes.setup: Warning about time limit for wearing the heavy hat
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation of serious permanent injury risk
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace safety - the casual tone about permanent injury

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We're told you have about five minutes before the heavy hat clips off your circulation.

dialogue.speaker: Camera operator
dialogue.line: Oh, okay.

jokes.text: Perhaps you have a bee in your bonnet. - Bee? [Screaming]
jokes.setup: Apu uses idiom about buzzing hat
jokes.punchline: Homer takes it literally and panics
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literal interpretation of idiom - good character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Homer, I'm as busy as a bee in a buzzing hat!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A bee?! In a hat?! Where?! Get it off! Get it off me!

jokes.text: He had one day left till retirement.
jokes.setup: Hat destroyed during Homer's bee panic
jokes.punchline: Parody of cop movie cliché about retiring officers
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference to action movie trope applied to surveillance equipment

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: Well, it's been a long road. Twenty-three years on the job, and I've seen things that would make a grown man cry. But today, I'm turning in my badge.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: I'm too old for this stuff.

jokes.text: Oh, no. It is encrusted with filth. Oh, well. Let's sell it anyway.
jokes.setup: Apu discovers filthy hot dog roller
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides to sell the contaminated food anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu character moment - acknowledging the problem but choosing profit anyway

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Oh my. This hot dog roller is filthy. But wait... if I clean it, I lose three minutes of sales. Eh, the health inspector is not due for another month.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I will simply sell the contaminated hot dogs at a slight discount.

jokes.text: Now, this is just between me and you, smashed hat.
jokes.setup: Apu about to admit wrongdoing
jokes.punchline: Confides in the destroyed surveillance equipment
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd moment - Apu treating the smashed hat as confidant shows his isolation and obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I must confess something to you. I have been... how do you say... cutting corners at the Kwik-E-Mart.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But do not worry, I am telling this only to you, my destroyed surveillance equipment. You cannot report me now!

jokes.text: Apu, will you ever stop selling spoiled meat? - No. I mean, yes. I mean-- Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Homer confronts Apu on camera about spoiled meat
jokes.punchline: Apu's flustered contradiction and realization he's been caught
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the investigation setup - Apu's nervous contradiction sells the moment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Apu, you've been selling spoiled meat to our town!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: No, no, no! The meat is fresh! I would never... Well, maybe once or twice, but only on Tuesdays!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Wait... oh no. The cameras are still rolling, aren't they?

jokes.text: I think I come off very well. - Monster! Run, children!
jokes.setup: Apu optimistically assesses his TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Immediate crowd reaction calling him a monster
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contrast between Apu's self-assessment and public reaction

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I am feeling very confident about my television appearance. I think I really connected with the audience.

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: Monster! Monster!

jokes.text: And if I can obtain for you these animals?
jokes.setup: Boss mentions scapegoat or sacrificial lamb
jokes.punchline: Apu offers to literally find these animals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal interpretation of metaphor - shows Apu's cultural disconnect and desperation

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: We need a scapegoat. Someone to be our sacrificial lamb.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I can get you a lamb. I have a guy. Also goats, no problem.

jokes.text: And you can no longer tell people about our fried pickles.
jokes.setup: Boss listing serious punishments for firing
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific restriction about fried pickles
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random corporate punishment - the specificity of fried pickles makes it absurdly funny

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You're fired! And as punishment, you're banned from all fried foods... except fried pickles. You may eat fried pickles on Tuesdays.

jokes.text: The other one too.
jokes.setup: Apu turns in pricing gun
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had multiple pricing guns hidden
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup - implies Apu was heavily armed with pricing equipment

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Here is my pricing gun.

dialogue.speaker: Corporate boss
dialogue.line: Thank you for turning that in.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Of course. I have nothing to hide.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Well, except for these seventeen pricing guns I have hidden around the store.

jokes.text: Give me that wiener! - No! I don't want to live, sir! - Give me that wiener! - No, Apu! It's not worth it!
jokes.setup: Apu contemplating suicide over job loss
jokes.punchline: Homer trying to take his hot dog during the crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his food obsession takes priority even during Apu's emotional breakdown

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I have lost everything. My job, my purpose, my will to live.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, that's rough buddy. Say, you gonna eat that hot dog?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Homer! I am contemplating ending it all and you want my food?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, no point letting it go to waste.

jokes.text: Even this babbling brook sounds almost like mocking laughter.
jokes.setup: Apu in despair, everything seems to mock him
jokes.punchline: The brook actually does sound like mocking laughter
jokes.explanation: Good setup for environmental gag - Apu's paranoia about mockery

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: The brook actually does sound like mocking laughter

jokes.text: Who needs money when we got feathers?
jokes.setup: Random hobo commenting on life philosophy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre logic about feathers replacing money
jokes.explanation: Solid background absurdity - random hobo wisdom that makes no sense

dialogue.speaker: Hobo
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about the economy. If feathers replaced money, we'd all be rich! Well, except for the chickens. They'd be broke.

jokes.text: It's true! It's true! We're so lame.
jokes.setup: Watching racist stand-up comedy on TV
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically agreeing with stereotype about white people
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer self-deprecating agreement with obvious stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Huh huh, that's true! We are pretty lazy!

jokes.text: No! Don't kill me! I didn't know there was film in that camera in that hat! I was unaware! I was unaware!
jokes.setup: Apu appears threatening in the storm
jokes.punchline: Homer's panicked confession about the surveillance
jokes.explanation: Good Homer panic moment - his immediate confession under perceived threat

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Please don't hurt me! I confess! I'm the one who keeps bumping into the surveillance camera at the Kwik-E-Mart!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Homer, I'm just trying to get home in this storm.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that's a relief. I thought you were going to kill me over all those times I've been caught on tape buying Squishees at 3 AM.

jokes.text: Now that I think about it, it may be a little confusing. Many have died needlessly.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining his village's apology pose
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of multiple deaths from misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark throwaway line - the casual tone about deadly cultural misunderstandings is perfectly Simpsons

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: In my country, we have an apology so elaborate, we have a whole department of people whose job it is to apologize.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, what do they apologize for?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Misunderstandings... it's killed dozens of us.

jokes.text: You can't sell that! Karma can only be portioned out by the cosmos. - He's got me there.
jokes.setup: Apu trying to sell karma as a concept
jokes.punchline: Lisa's philosophical objection completely defeats him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - her precocious philosophical knowledge immediately trumps Apu's sales pitch

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: You see, Lisa, karma is a beautiful concept. What goes around comes around.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But Apu, if karma is real, how do you explain all the bad people who prosper and good people who suffer?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Well, you see, it's not always immediate. Karma works in mysterious ways across lifetimes and—

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: So it's unfalsifiable. You can't prove it wrong because you can always claim it's working in ways we can't see. That's not philosophy, that's just faith.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I... well... yes. Yes, I suppose you're right.

jokes.text: Okay, okay. I'll let him. But then I get a Chipwich. Okay?
jokes.setup: Family discussing letting Apu help as penance
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely unrelated demand for ice cream sandwich
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur - connecting serious family discussion to his snack desires

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Fine, Apu can help as penance. But first, I want an ice cream sandwich.

jokes.text: I can see through time.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries Apu's extremely spicy food
jokes.punchline: Claims supernatural powers from the heat
jokes.explanation: Great spicy food reaction - the specific claim of time vision is wonderfully absurd

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Wow, Apu, this is incredibly spicy!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, it is authentic Indian cuisine. Very hot!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I can see sounds and hear colors. I think I'm levitating. Do I have the power to move objects with my mind?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: No, that is just the capsaicin.

jokes.text: For instance, tonight I'm using a-- Apu, what do you call this thing again? - A napkin. - Outrageous!
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to be worldly about trying new things
jokes.punchline: The 'exotic' new thing is just a napkin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his amazement at basic civilization combined with his attempt to sound sophisticated

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm a man of the world. I try new things all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, today I tried something very exotic.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A napkin.

jokes.text: Because I like people, and I need a place where I'll be out of the sun.
jokes.setup: Standard job interview question about motivation
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest and minimal qualifications
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of typical job interview answers - refreshingly honest low standards

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: So tell me, what motivates you to work here?

dialogue.speaker: Job applicant
dialogue.line: I need money to buy things.

jokes.text: Wait-Wait a minute! Those aren't convenience stores. That sounds like the resume of a Hollywood movie star. - Uh, yeah, well-- Huh! [Both Gasp] James Woods!
jokes.setup: James Woods listing movie titles as job experience
jokes.punchline: Manager recognizes him as actual celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo setup - the recognition scene works well

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: I've done Videodrome, The Boost, Salvador...

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: Wait, you're James Woods!

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: The actor?

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: You're hired!

jokes.text: And in the film Chaplin-- I had a little cameo in that-- I actually traveled in time back to the '20s where-- Well, [Chuckles] I-I've said too much.
jokes.setup: James Woods explaining his method acting research
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of actual time travel, then catching himself
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of method acting absurdity - time travel as casual research method

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: For my roles, I do extensive research. I actually went back in time to study the 1920s for my gangster picture.

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: I mean... I read a lot of books about the 1920s.

jokes.text: What the-- - Uh, I like to keep a lollipop there.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers Apu somewhere unexpected in his house
jokes.punchline: Apu's casual explanation for hiding candy in bizarre location
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Apu moment - the casual tone about inappropriate candy storage

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Apu! What are you doing in my attic?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Oh, hello Homer. I was just storing some of my candy inventory up here. You know how it is - the Kwik-E-Mart gets so crowded, I need overflow storage.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: In MY attic?!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, well, your attic maintains the perfect temperature and humidity for preserving the structural integrity of my Squishees. Plus, the rent is reasonable - one free Slurpee per month.

jokes.text: Apu, we usually store our cans in the cupboard. - Oh, they'll never move that way.
jokes.setup: Marge correcting Apu's unconventional can storage
jokes.punchline: Apu applies retail logic to home pantry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu character work - his inability to separate retail mindset from domestic life

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Apu, you can't just stack cans like that. They'll fall over.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But Mrs. Simpson, at the Kwik-E-Mart we use the pyramid method. It maximizes shelf space and creates an attractive display.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: This isn't a store, Apu. It's a pantry.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, and if I arrange them properly, I can fit 40% more inventory and charge myself a markup.

jokes.text: I cannot go there. That is the scene of my spiritual depantsing.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests going to Kwik-E-Mart
jokes.punchline: Apu's poetic description of his humiliation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'spiritual depantsing' is a wonderfully creative way to describe professional humiliation

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Ah, yes, the Kwik-E-Mart. Where I spend my days in quiet desperation, watching the world pass by through bullet-proof glass, like a man observing life from inside a fishbowl of broken dreams and expired milk.

jokes.text: Ooh! Great selection and rock-bottom prices. But where is the love?
jokes.setup: Apu evaluating the Monstromart's business model
jokes.punchline: Missing the human element despite efficiency
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Apu values personal service over corporate efficiency

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, the Kwik-E-Mart operates with supreme efficiency. Every item is precisely catalogued, every transaction optimized.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But what is profit without purpose? What is commerce without compassion? I have forgotten why I opened this store.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I came to America with nothing but a dream and a suitcase. Now I have much, but I have lost the human element.

jokes.text: Attention, Monstromart shoppers. Just a reminder that we love each and every one of you. - Aww!
jokes.setup: Apu just complained about lack of love at big store
jokes.punchline: Store immediately provides generic artificial love message
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback showing the artificial nature of corporate 'love'

dialogue.speaker: PA Announcer
dialogue.line: Attention shoppers, we love you.

jokes.text: Oh! I've killed her! It's all happening again!
jokes.setup: Apu accidentally knocks over store employee
jokes.punchline: Assumes he's killed her and references a pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Apu's guilt complex - implies this has happened before

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Oh no! I have killed the clerk! And I am so very sorry! But I have killed many people before, so this is okay.

jokes.text: Help me! Help me! [Gurgling] Mmm! It's cran-tastic!
jokes.setup: Employee appears to be drowning in cranberry sauce
jokes.punchline: Delivers enthusiastic product endorsement while drowning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark corporate satire - maintaining product enthusiasm even while dying

dialogue.speaker: Store Employee
dialogue.line: This cranberry sauce is so delicious, I'm literally drowning in it! Five stars!

jokes.text: Uh, Mrs. Simpson, the express line is the fastest line not always. That old man up front, he is starved for attention. He will talk the cashier's head off.
jokes.setup: Marge choosing express checkout line
jokes.punchline: Apu's retail expertise about customer psychology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu expertise - his deep understanding of customer behavior and checkout dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Ah yes, the express lane. You see, we strategically place the candy and magazines at eye level to trigger impulse purchases. The checkout counter is deliberately slow to create anxiety, which makes customers more likely to buy items they don't need. And these dividers? They're not for organization—they're psychological barriers that make customers feel trapped, increasing their stress and spending.

jokes.text: I got up in the morning and made myself a piece of toast. I set the toaster to three-- medium brown.
jokes.setup: Old man starting what should be a simple story
jokes.punchline: Excruciating detail about mundane toast preparation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Apu's warning - perfectly tedious old person storytelling

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Back in my day, I walked uphill both ways to school. Speaking of which, this morning I made toast. Now, the toaster was a General Electric model from 1987, chrome finish with a slight dent on the left side from when I dropped it in '94. I had to adjust the dial—it was set to 3, but I needed it at 2.5 for the perfect golden brown. The bread was Wonder Bread, slightly stale, which actually works better because it doesn't burn as easily. I inserted the first slice at 8:47 AM, and it took exactly 2 minutes and 14 seconds to pop up. The butter I used was salted, not unsalted, because unsalted is for people who hate flavor and America.

jokes.text: Yes, but look-- all pathetic single men. Only cash, no chitchat.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining why the longest line is actually fastest
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of lonely men's shopping efficiency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor - dark but accurate assessment of different customer types and their checkout behavior

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: You see, the longest line moves the fastest. All these lonely men, they have nowhere to be. They shop slowly, carefully reading each label. But me? I have a family waiting at home. I am efficient. In and out, seven minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: These men, they linger. They compare prices. They ask questions. But a man with a family, he knows what he needs. Milk, eggs, bread. Done. While they are still reading the fine print on a can of beans, I am already home.

jokes.text: Did I sound like a real Kwik-E-Mart, you know, kinda guy? - Actually, I thought it was a little labored.
jokes.setup: James Woods seeking feedback on his convenience store performance
jokes.punchline: Customer gives honest critical review
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about method acting - celebrity getting honest feedback from regular person

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: So, how am I doing as a convenience store clerk?

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Honestly? You're terrible. You're slow, rude, and you keep trying to pitch your movies to customers.

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Well, I won a Golden Globe!

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: For what? 'Worst Employee of the Year'?

jokes.text: You gotta lose yourself in the moment, man. - I'm me? - Hey, don't jerk me around, fella.
jokes.setup: Customer giving acting advice to James Woods
jokes.punchline: Woods' confusion about basic identity in method acting
jokes.explanation: Good method acting satire - Woods overthinking simple customer service

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: You know, James, the key to method acting is really getting into character. You have to become that person.

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Become them? What do you mean? I'm already me.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: No, no, you have to inhabit their mind, their soul, their very being.

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: But... if I become them, then who am I? Where do I go?

jokes.text: That's even worse than the album Grampa released.
jokes.setup: Lisa playing terrible shehnai music
jokes.punchline: Homer references Grampa's equally terrible musical effort
jokes.explanation: Good background family history gag - implies Grampa's musical ambitions

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Lisa, your shehnai playing is almost as bad as the time Grampa tried to play the accordion.

jokes.text: [Beating] [Electrical Crackling] [Slowly] Ahh. Apu friend me good.
jokes.setup: Homer eating healthy vegetarian food
jokes.punchline: His heart literally rebooting like a machine
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about Homer's poor health - heart literally restarting from vegetables

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... wait, this is good for me? But it tastes like... salad.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, the doctor said you need to eat healthier.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But Marge, my heart needs donuts, not... vegetables.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm actually feeling... better? What's happening to my body?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It feels like my heart is... rebooting. Beep boop beep!

jokes.text: You see, whether igloo, hut or lean-to... or a geodesic dome... there's no structure I have been to which I'd rather call my home.
jokes.setup: Apu begins heartfelt speech about feeling at home with Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate architectural terminology for simple sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Apu's tendency toward verbose, formal speech

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: You know, I feel like I'm truly domiciled here with you Simpson family. It's like the load-bearing wall of my emotional infrastructure has finally found its proper foundation.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Uh, Apu, you just live next door.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, but the perpendicular proximity of our dwellings has created a paradigm of spatial-affective symbiosis!

jokes.text: Sorry 'bout the salmonella - Heh-heh! That's okay.
jokes.setup: Apu singing apology song to Homer
jokes.punchline: Casually apologizes for giving him food poisoning, Homer accepts
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the episode's inciting incident wrapped in musical number

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry I gave you food poisoning, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, that's okay, Apu.

jokes.text: Ahh. Everything really wrapped up nicely. Hmm. Much quicker than usual.
jokes.setup: Family musical number ending
jokes.punchline: Homer commenting on the episode's pacing and resolution
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on sitcom structure - Homer aware of narrative pacing

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Whew! That was a good episode. Nothing left to resolve.

jokes.text: Hey, he's not happy at all! He lied to us through song!
jokes.setup: Discovering Apu is still sad after the happy musical number
jokes.punchline: Homer's outrage at being deceived by musical theater conventions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about musical theater - Homer's literal interpretation of song as deception is wonderfully naive

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute! You're still sad?! But you just sang and danced!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: In musical theater, when you sing and dance, your problems go away!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I've been lied to!

jokes.text: I hate when people do that!
jokes.setup: Homer's outrage about lying through song
jokes.punchline: Acts like this is a common problem he faces
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up - implies Homer has been frequently deceived by musical numbers

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why does everyone always expect me to lie through song? It happens all the time!

jokes.text: I hate when people do that!
jokes.setup: Homer's outrage about musical deception
jokes.punchline: He acts like this is a common problem
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Homer treating this very specific situation as if it happens all the time. Nice character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Musical deception?! That happens all the time! Why, just last week I thought I was listening to The Beatles, but it turned out to be The Monkees!

jokes.text: Well, the least I could do is absolutely nothing. But I'll go you one better and come along.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he'll help Apu get his job back
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that helping is above his minimum effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - redefining helping as going above his baseline of doing nothing

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Look, Apu, I'd love to help you get your job back, but helping is above my minimum effort.

jokes.text: That's over 16,000 kilometers. - D'oh!
jokes.setup: Lisa converting distance to metric
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated reaction to metric system
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer confusion about metric system - good character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: The metric system is based on powers of 10, Dad. It's much more logical than our customary system.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Logical? Lisa, the only 'system' I understand is the one where you give me donuts and I give you nothing!

jokes.text: Are we in India yet? - No. - Oh, wait... now we are.
jokes.setup: Long journey to India
jokes.punchline: Immediate transition from asking to arriving
jokes.explanation: Good time compression gag - typical Simpsons travel montage efficiency

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So Apu, how long will it take to get to India?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Oh, about 24 hours if we fly non-stop.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: 24 hours? That's forever!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Well, we're here.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What? Already?

jokes.text: How can it be the same movie if they've changed my character from a tightly wound convenience-store clerk to a jittery Eskimo firefighter?
jokes.setup: James Woods discussing movie role changes
jokes.punchline: Completely unrelated character transformation
jokes.explanation: Great Hollywood satire - absurd character change from convenience clerk to Eskimo firefighter

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: I just got offered the role of a lifetime! They want me to play a sensitive, introspective farmer who tends to his crops and contemplates the meaning of life.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: That doesn't sound like you at all.

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: I know! But I'm going to take it. I'm going to completely transform myself. New wardrobe, new accent, new everything!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Wow, that's really impressive. So when does this new role start?

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Well, it already has. I'm now a turnip farmer from Manitoba.

jokes.text: Why, you damn, frickin', no good mother-- [Beeping] cheese! No, not you. I'm just talkin' to my oven.
jokes.setup: James Woods getting angry on phone
jokes.punchline: Censors himself mid-swear, then covers with oven excuse
jokes.explanation: Good TV censorship gag - creative swear word substitution and absurd excuse

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: What do you mean my check bounced?! That's bull—BLEEP! I mean, the oven! The oven is making that noise!

jokes.text: Oh, great. Christians.
jokes.setup: Religious singing on airplane
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual religious intolerance
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer religious insensitivity - economical character moment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why is God making me sit through this?

dialogue.speaker: Flanders
dialogue.line: It's beautiful, Homer. A celebration of faith.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, your God can take a hike. My God is in a bottle.

jokes.text: This isn't very convenient. - Must you dump on everything we do?
jokes.setup: Visiting the world's first convenience store
jokes.punchline: Homer points out it's actually inconvenient
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation - the first convenience store being inconvenient

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... if this is the world's first convenience store, how come it's so inconvenient? I had to walk all the way here, there's no parking, and they don't have a hot dog roller.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But sir, it is convenient compared to having no store at all.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Convenient? I've been more convenient at the DMV!

jokes.text: You may ask me three questions. - That's great, because all I need is one. - Are you really the head of the Kwik-E-Mart? - Yes. - Really? - Yes. - You? - Yes. I hope this has been enlightening for you.
jokes.setup: Mystical three questions setup
jokes.punchline: Homer wastes all three asking the same thing differently
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - perfectly wastes the mystical opportunity through his literal-mindedness and inability to believe

dialogue.speaker: Kwik-E-Mart President
dialogue.line: You have three questions.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Okay. Will I ever be rich?

dialogue.speaker: Kwik-E-Mart President
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Will I ever be wealthy?

dialogue.speaker: Kwik-E-Mart President
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Will I ever have a lot of money?

dialogue.speaker: Kwik-E-Mart President
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Is he really the head of the Kwik-E-Mart? - [Angry Groan]
jokes.setup: Homer asks the same question again to Apu
jokes.punchline: Apu's frustrated response to Homer's continued obtuseness
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer still doesn't understand what happened

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying the Kwik-E-Mart is open 24 hours?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, Homer, we have been through this. Every day, you come in and ask me the same question.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But when do you close?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: We do not close! Ever! I haven't slept in 12 years!

jokes.text: Do you mind? I'm not in the mood.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to cheer up Apu with their song
jokes.punchline: Apu rejecting his own theme song when depressed
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Apu's mood has changed - rejects his own anthem

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Apu. I'll cheer you up with a song! Apu, Apu, Apu, Apu, Apu, Apu...

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Please, Homer, not now. I'm not in the mood.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Come on, it's your theme song!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I don't care. Just leave me alone.

jokes.text: I've learned that life is one crushing defeat after another... until you just wish Flanders was dead.
jokes.setup: Homer attempts to console Apu with life wisdom
jokes.punchline: His philosophy devolves into personal grudge against neighbor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - failed wisdom that reveals his pettiness and nihilism

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Apu, listen to me. Life is full of disappointments. You just gotta accept that and move on.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Thank you, Homer. That is very wise.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, that's what I always say. Like when Flanders got that new boat and I didn't. Sure, it hurt. But I accepted it.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Then he had the nerve to use it every single weekend, showing it off, waving at me. WHO DOES THAT? Who waves at someone from their boat?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That smug, stupid face... I'm gonna key that boat.

jokes.text: I have learned that life is one crushing defeat after another until you just wish Flanders was dead.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to offer philosophical comfort
jokes.punchline: Ends profound statement with petty neighbor hatred
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Homer philosophy - starts profound and universal, ends petty and specific. Perfect character integration and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, son, life is about the journey, not the destination. It's about appreciating the moment and finding peace within yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Of course, none of that applies to Flanders. That guy can rot in hell.

jokes.text: Oh, yes. It was horrible, I tell you. By the end, I thought I was a hummingbird of some kind.
jokes.setup: Apu recounting working 96 hours straight
jokes.punchline: Hallucinated being a hummingbird from exhaustion
jokes.explanation: Great specific absurd detail - hummingbird is perfect choice for sleep-deprived hallucination

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I have been working 96 hours straight.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I hallucinated I was a hummingbird.

jokes.text: In a few minutes I try to drink nectar out of Sanjay's head.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining his hummingbird hallucination
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about trying to feed from coworker's head
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the hummingbird gag - specific and visual, perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I was trying to feed from Squishy's head, but he kept swatting me away.

jokes.text: In a few minutes I try to drink nectar out of Sanjay's head.
jokes.setup: Apu mentioned thinking he was a hummingbird
jokes.punchline: Describes acting on the hallucination with coworker
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on hummingbird hallucination with logical absurd escalation

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I thought I was a hummingbird, so I tried to fly out the Kwik-E-Mart window.

dialogue.speaker: Sanjay
dialogue.line: And I had to tackle you before you hurt yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, thank you for that, Sanjay. For a moment, I was living the dream.

jokes.text: Hey, pal. I assure you. If I tried any funny stuff, you would be in hysterics.
jokes.setup: Robber tells him not to try funny stuff
jokes.punchline: Woods takes it literally as comedy performance
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay - James Woods interpreting criminal threat as comedy critique

dialogue.speaker: Robber
dialogue.line: Don't try anything funny.

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Oh, you want me to do my act? Alright, alright. So I'm walking down the street the other day, and I see this guy with a parrot on his shoulder. I say to him, 'Hey buddy, where'd you get the monkey?' He says, 'This isn't a monkey, it's a parrot!' I say, 'I know, I was talking to the parrot!'

jokes.text: Well, Mr. Woods... your new song is gonna be number three with a bullet. - I-I'm not a singer.
jokes.setup: Robber making threat with gun
jokes.punchline: Woods corrects music industry terminology confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay - 'number three with a bullet' works as both chart position and death threat

dialogue.speaker: Robber
dialogue.line: This is a robbery! Give me all your money or I'll shoot!

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. You can't just 'shoot' in this context. You need to say you're going to 'sample' my wallet without permission. That's how the music industry works.

jokes.text: The searing kiss of hot lead. How I've missed you. I mean, I think I'm dying.
jokes.setup: Apu gets shot saving James Woods
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of gunshot, then catches himself
jokes.explanation: Good Apu character moment - his poetic nature even when shot, then practical reality

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: As I fell to the ground, time seemed to slow. The bullet had pierced my flesh like a dove through a cloud of spun sugar... Wait, no. I was shot.

jokes.text: Well, you are a very lucky man, Apu. You see, the bullet ricocheted off another bullet that was lodged in your chest from a previous robbery.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining why Apu survived gunshot
jokes.punchline: Previous bullet already lodged in chest deflected new one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist medical explanation - implies Apu gets shot regularly enough to build armor

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, it appears the previous bullet already lodged in his chest deflected the new one.

jokes.text: But as for me, I'm off to battle aliens on a faraway planet. - That sounds like a good movie. - Yes. Yes, a-a movie. Yes.
jokes.setup: James Woods describing his next project
jokes.punchline: Awkward confirmation it's definitely just a movie
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity departure gag - implies James Woods might actually be fighting aliens

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: My next project is a biopic about a convenience store clerk who struggles with the daily grind of retail work.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: That's... that's my life.

dialogue.speaker: James Woods
dialogue.line: Yes, well, it's a movie.

tmdb_overview: Homer accompanies Apu to the Kwik-E-Mart home office--in India--after Apu is fired.
insight: Homer's food poisoning spawns 80 jokes in 22 minutes—absurdist escalation at its densest.
